title: Marketing-sourced deals close 14 days faster this week
anchor: Current week marketing wins average 38 days to close versus the 11-week baseline of 52 days.
connect: Seven of nine wins this week came from mid-market accounts.
body: Current week marketing wins average 38 days to close versus the 11-week baseline of 52 days. Seven of nine wins this week came from mid-market accounts.
trace: Deal velocity acceleration signal from current week marketing-sourced closes showing 27% faster cycle time.

title: Paid social flipped channel mix this quarter
anchor: Q2 paid social created $620K in pipeline versus paid search at $380K.
connect: Q1 ran the opposite direction with paid search at $720K and paid social at $410K.
body: Q2 paid social created $620K in pipeline versus paid search at $380K. Q1 ran the opposite direction with paid search at $720K and paid social at $410K.
trace: Channel performance reversal between Q1 and Q2 with paid social gaining $210K while paid search dropped $340K.

title: Mid-market SQL volume up 50% in April
anchor: April mid-market SQLs at 147 compared to the Jan-Mar monthly average of 98.
connect: ABM acceptance rate runs at 61% versus 42% for non-ABM leads this month.
body: April mid-market SQLs at 147 compared to the Jan-Mar monthly average of 98. ABM acceptance rate runs at 61% versus 42% for non-ABM leads this month.
trace: Mid-market lead generation acceleration with ABM showing 19-point acceptance rate advantage over general funnel.

title: Branded search doubled over six weeks
anchor: Weekly branded search volume climbed from 1,000 to 1,921 with growth every week.
connect: Cumulative growth hit 92% across the six-week window ending April 19.
body: Weekly branded search volume climbed from 1,000 to 1,921 with growth every week. Cumulative growth hit 92% across the six-week window ending April 19.
trace: Consistent branded search momentum with zero down weeks and near-doubling of weekly volume.

title: Direct traffic crossed organic for the first time
anchor: April traffic share sits at 42% direct versus 37% organic search.
connect: Direct first overtook organic on a cumulative basis on April 15.
body: April traffic share sits at 42% direct versus 37% organic search. Direct first overtook organic on a cumulative basis on April 15.
trace: Traffic composition milestone with direct becoming the primary channel for the first time.

title: Enterprise win rate jumped to 31% in Q2
anchor: Q2 enterprise deals close at 31% win rate with average deal size at $187K.
connect: Trailing four quarters ran at 22% win rate with $142K average deal size.
body: Q2 enterprise deals close at 31% win rate with average deal size at $187K. Trailing four quarters ran at 22% win rate with $142K average deal size.
trace: Enterprise segment performance improvement with 9-point win rate increase and $45K ASP uplift.

title: Event-sourced deals accelerated 24 days this quarter
anchor: Q1 event wins averaged 46 days to close versus Q4 at 69 days.
connect: SaaS Connect delivered all 18 Q1 event-sourced wins.
body: Q1 event wins averaged 46 days to close versus Q4 at 69 days. SaaS Connect delivered all 18 Q1 event-sourced wins.
trace: Event channel velocity improvement with SaaS Connect showing consistent conversion pattern.

title: Atlas outpaces top competitor in mentions by 52%
anchor: April Atlas mentions at 1,840 versus Beacon Systems at 1,210.
connect: Analyst mentions doubled to 9 in the last 14 days versus 6 in the prior 30.
body: April Atlas mentions at 1,840 versus Beacon Systems at 1,210. Analyst mentions doubled to 9 in the last 14 days versus 6 in the prior 30.
trace: Brand visibility advantage over primary competitor with acceleration in analyst coverage frequency.

title: Fintech closed deals concentrate in mid-market this quarter
anchor: Last 60 days mid-market wins include 10 fintech accounts out of 13 total.
connect: All 13 mid-market wins run Snowflake plus dbt in their stack.
body: Last 60 days mid-market wins include 10 fintech accounts out of 13 total. All 13 mid-market wins run Snowflake plus dbt in their stack.
trace: Vertical and tech stack concentration pattern in recent mid-market closed-won deals.

title: Digital ads spend sits $84K under plan at mid-quarter
anchor: Q2 digital ads actual spend at $256K versus $340K planned through April 23.
connect: The $84K reallocated to SaaS Connect and Signal Summit events.
body: Q2 digital ads actual spend at $256K versus $340K planned through April 23. The $84K reallocated to SaaS Connect and Signal Summit events.
trace: Budget reallocation from digital channels to field events mid-quarter.

title: SDR capacity runs ahead of inbound volume this week
anchor: Current week SDR capacity at 210 versus inbound lead volume at 142.
connect: Trailing four weeks averaged capacity 204 with volume at 194.
body: Current week SDR capacity at 210 versus inbound lead volume at 142. Trailing four weeks averaged capacity 204 with volume at 194.
trace: SDR capacity utilization shift with current week showing 32% headroom versus recent average of 5%.

title: Atlas Insights reference opt-in rate leads product suite
anchor: Atlas Insights opt-ins at 75% versus Atlas Workflow at 72% and Atlas Connect at 28%.
connect: Connect sits 44 points below the other two products in reference willingness.
body: Atlas Insights opt-ins at 75% versus Atlas Workflow at 72% and Atlas Connect at 28%. Connect sits 44 points below the other two products in reference willingness.
trace: Product-level reference participation rates showing significant variance across the Atlas suite.

title: Three named accounts hit high-intent threshold this week
anchor: Current week target accounts with 5+ high-intent events total 3 out of 50 universe.
connect: March ABM Add list contributed 1 account while Named Accounts delivered 2.
body: Current week target accounts with 5+ high-intent events total 3 out of 50 universe. March ABM Add list contributed 1 account while Named Accounts delivered 2.
trace: Named account engagement spike with 6% of target universe crossing intent threshold simultaneously.

title: Atlas Assist launch locks calendar tomorrow
anchor: Atlas Assist ships May 8 with campaign assets ready and calendar locking April 24.
connect: Launch readiness sits at green status across campaign workstreams.
body: Atlas Assist ships May 8 with campaign assets ready and calendar locking April 24. Launch readiness sits at green status across campaign workstreams.
trace: Product launch coordination milestone with marketing campaign assets complete ahead of calendar lock.

title: Forrester and G2 split recent analyst coverage
anchor: Last 14 days analyst mentions include Forrester at 3 and G2 at 3 out of 9 total.
connect: Gartner contributed 1 mention in the same window.
body: Last 14 days analyst mentions include Forrester at 3 and G2 at 3 out of 9 total. Gartner contributed 1 mention in the same window.
trace: Analyst coverage distribution showing equal representation from two key research firms.
